rulings.Case Title: JOEL JENNINGS WARNE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A LOCAL PUBLIC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For: Sanctions Pursuant To, Inter Alia, C.C.P. 128.7(C)(1) And Civ. Code 3294 In Re: False Representations; Reconsideration Pursuant To, Inter Alia, C.C.P. 1008, Et Seq.; Declaratory Judgment Clarifying Scope Of Orders; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of The Same.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 7, 2018, Line 17, PETITIONER JOEL WARNE Motion For: Sanctions Pursuant To, Inter Alia, C.C.P. 128.7(C)(1) And Civ. Code 3294 In Re: False Representations; Reconsideration Pursuant To, Inter Alia, C.C.P. 1008, Et Seq.; Declaratory Judgment Clarifying Scope Of Orders; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of The Same. Petitioner Joel Warne's motion for sanctions and reconsideration of the order dated May 10, 2017 is denied in its entirety. As to the portion of the motion seeking sanctions, Mr. Warne fails to show that CCSF engaged in conduct that is frivolous, improper, or "indisputably without merit." Mr. Warne complains that CCSF failed to produce his "Discharge Summary." CCSF more accurately refers to this document as discharge instructions. Mr. Warne never signed the document. CCSF explains that when a patient does not sign the instructions, they do not become part of a patient's medical records. (O'Donnell Dec., pars. 6-9.) Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to show that Mr. Warne did not receive a complete set of his medical records. For the same reason, there is no basis to reconsider the May 10, 2017 order and the request to do so is untimely per CCP 1008. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, AS SUBROGEE TO VS. TRITON SUBS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Triton Subs, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Seven Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Triton Subs, Inc., In The Amount Of 1,050.00
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, May 7, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, AS SUBROGEE TO, QFA ROYALTIES LLC, QUIZNOS FRANCHISING II, LLC and QIP HOLDER, LLC's Motion To Compel Defendant Triton Subs, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Seven Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Triton Subs, Inc., In The Amount Of 1,050.00 Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories nos. 72, 73, 74, 75. 76 & 78 is granted as follows; 1. Defendants' objections are overruled. To the extent that defendant limited its responses based upon these objection, it will deliver to plaintiff full and complete responses within thirty (30) days from the date of this order; to the extent that it continues to withhold any responsive information on the basis of privilege it will identify the basis for such claim with sufficient particularity to allow plaintiff to challenge the assertion of privilege. 2. Defendant is ordered to respond without objection to interrogatory 78 within the time period specified in paragraph (1) above. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,000. Will be assessed against defendant with payment to be made to plaintiff within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Counsel for plaintiff should prepare an appropriate order, submit to counsel for defendant for approval as to form and deliver to the court at the hearing on May 7, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/jpt

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 7, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF ALAMEDA'S, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant County of Alameda's demurrer to all causes of action alleged against it in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Tylon Hunt is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all causes of action. California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings supports granting Mr. Hunt's request to file a second amended complaint. rulings.Case Title: JACK H REISNER VS. UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS AND JOINERS OF AM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 7, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS, DOUG MCCLAFFERTY, UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS NATIONAL JOB'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants UBC, Doug McClafferty and Job Corps' demurrer to all three causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jack Reisner is: a) is overruled as to the first cause of action for common law discrimination and retaliation against public policy against UBC; b) sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action against Job Corps; c) overruled as to the second cause of action for violation of Labor Code 1102.5; d) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third case of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress against UBC and Mr. McClaferty; and e) sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action against Job Corps. Liberally construed, the first cause of action alleges sufficient facts to support a wrongful termination claim against UBC based on Mr. Reisner's repeated requests for reasonable disability accommodation and his complaint about the treatment of minor students to an outside authority, but is untimely against JOB Corps. Mr. Reisner has sufficiently pled that UBC was his employer. The second cause of action is timely because it does not seek civil penalties (Ayala v. Frito Lay, Inc. (E.D. Cal. 2017) 263 F.Supp.3d 891, 917) and, liberally construed, alleges sufficient facts to support a violation of section1102.5 because the reasonableness of Mr. Reisner's belief in the illegality of defendants' actions is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Resnick is given leave to allege facts showing harassment on the basis of disability which can support an IIED cause of action. An IIED claim is time-barred as to Job Corps. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Reisner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KOVACS VS. AVG TECHNOLOGIES USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Third Party The Boston Consulting Group To Produce Documents Pursuant To Properly Served Subpoena Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, may 7, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF GARY KOVACS' Motion For An Order Compelling Third Party The Boston Consulting Group To Produce Documents Pursuant To Properly Served Subpoena Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Gary Kovac's motion to compel non-party Boston Consulting Group, Inc, ("BCG") to produce documents responsive to the subpoena served on it is granted. The court is prepared to sign the proposed order submitted by plaintiff. At the hearing on May 7, 2018 counsel for plaintiff and BCG should be prepared to address the date by which the documents shall be produced to plaintiff and the amount of monetary sanctions, if any, to be assessed against bcg. Although not required to do so due to the late filing, the court has received and reviewed the opposition filed by non-party and served by mail on May 1, 2018 for next day delivery. There is nothing in that opposition which justifies the delay in production nor grounds for this court to limit the requested production. More than thirty days after service of the subpoena on march 12, 2o18 counsel for BCG stated that BCG would produce all non-privileged materials that are responsive and provide a privilege log to the extent that any materials were withheld. (exhibit 6, p.40 to decl. of Mark Romeo. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. SHS CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 7, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication on its sole cause of action for money due on dishonor of check against defendants Paul Han Kim and In Ju Kim is denied. Wells Fargo has provided insufficient evidence to satisfy its burden of showing that it is a holder in due course. In particular, Wells Fargo fails to provide any evidence when it paid any money on the check, what it had done before doing so, whether it had any notice that the check might be dishonored, and why it chose not to place a hold on the check, if in fact it did not place a hold on the check. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROSS PSYHOGIOS VS. JEROME L. VITENSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, may 7, 2018, Line 4, Plaintiff Ross Psyhogios' motion to quash defendants Jerome Vitenson and C. Cournale and Co. Inc.'s deposition subpoena Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Ross Psyhogios' motion to quash defendants Jerome Vitenson and C. Cournale and Co. Inc.'s deposition subpoena is denied; his motion to modify the subpoena limiting the production to "medical records of plaintiff's respiratory condition including, but not limited to, chest x-rays, chest CT scans, chest imaging, allergy tests, evaluation for allergies, pulmonary test, pulmonary functioning, asthma testing, evaluation for asthma, and any conditions of coughing, wheezing, nasal congestion or difficulty breathing" is granted. There is insufficient evidence at this point that plaintiff is claiming any medical injury beyond his respiratory claims to justify the invasion of his privacy sought by defendant. No monetary sanctions will be imposed. Counsel for plaintiff should prepare an appropriate order, submit to counsel for defendants for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court for signing at the hearing on May 7, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
